news_items.headline: Cox, longtime Braves manager, WS champ, dies
news_items.summary: Bobby Cox, longtime manager of the Atlanta Braves and a World Series champion in 1995, has died at the age of 84.

news_items.headline: Guardians land Gold Glove C Bailey from Giants
news_items.summary: The Guardians have acquired catcher Patrick Bailey from the Giants in return for the No. 29 pick in this year's MLB draft and pitching prospect Matt Wilkinson.

news_items.headline: Brewers' Lockridge to IL after slamming into wall
news_items.summary: Brewers outfielder Brandon Lockridge has been placed on the injured list after he crashed into the side wall while chasing a foul ball.

news_items.headline: Historic heat: Misiorowski tops 103 mph 10 times
news_items.summary: Brewers right-hander Jacob Misiorowski threw 10 pitches of at least 103 mph against the Yankees and produced the highest velocity any starter has reached since Statcast tracking began in 2008.

news_items.headline: Sources: Dodgers' Snell (shoulder) to debut Sat.
news_items.summary: Tyler Glasnow was placed on the injured list because of what the Dodgers described as back spasms Friday, one day before fellow frontline starting pitcher Blake Snell is set to be activated for his season debut.

news_items.headline: Cubs win 10 in a row, again, in first since 1935
news_items.summary: Make it 10 wins in a row for the Chicago Cubs again. That's twice in one season -- for the first time in nine decades.

news_items.headline: Hall of Fame hitter Boggs says he's cancer-free
news_items.summary: Baseball Hall of Famer Wade Boggs says he has been declared cancer-free two years after announcing that he had been diagnosed with prostate cancer.

news_items.headline: Last-place Twins put top pitcher Taj Bradley on injured list with right pectoral inflammation
news_items.summary: Bradley has been Minnesota's lone pitching standout this season with a 2.87 ERA and strikeout rate of 10 per nine innings.

news_items.summary: TODAY’S GAME: The Houston Astros (16-23) and Cincinnati Reds (20-19) play their second game of a three-game series this afternoon at Great American Ball Park with first pitch scheduled for 3:10 p.m. CT.
